LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS MARY M. FONG, BASALLE WONG, AND BASALLE WONG REALTY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. RULING: AS TO THE DEMURRER, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE REMAINDER - OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12 AND THE PORTION OF THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION PERTAINING TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941.3. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 8, AND THE PORTION OF THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION PERTAINING TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3. AS TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE MOOT AS TO PARAGRAPHS 22, 23, AND 35; GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 43, 44, AND 49; DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 30, 48, AND 67; GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 4 OF THE PRAYER RE THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN #5220

Demurrer Of Defendants Mary M Fong, Etc To Complaint *** Continued From 7/7/05 To 7/8/05.
Off Calendar Jul-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARY M. FONG, BASALLE WONG, AND BASALLE WONG REALTY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCE: Gilbert Y. Jay, Esq. 788-7300 for defts & moving parties; no other appearances. (Note: Counsel stated that the tentative ruling was late & therefore per tentative ruling rule, did not have to give notice to the other side that he would appear.) (Note: Tentative ruling was recorded at 5:00pm.)
RULING: As to the demurrer, the Court modified its tentative ruling as to the 4th cause of action for constructive eviction and sustained without leave to amend. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as to the remainder - OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12 AND THE PORTION OF THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION PERTAINING TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941.3. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 8, AND THE PORTION OF THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION PERTAINING TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3. As to the motion to strike, the Court ruled as follows: motion to strike moot as to paragraphs 22, 23, and 35; granted as to paragraphs 43, 44, and 49; denied as to paragraphs 30, 48, and 67; granted as to paragraph 4 of the prayer re the 5th and 6th causes of action. Order signed in open court. (301/PHA/GY(EX)) Court Reporter Robert Balian, CSR #5220

Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint
